mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an approaching recession, triggering risk-off positioning and negative equity returns as investors price in lower future earnings.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with stable housing construction indicate robust aggregate demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both proxies rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production peaks above 103 during a late-cycle environment, supply constraints begin to clash with softening demand, compressing profit margins and generating negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when INDPRO crosses the 103 threshold, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly lifts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive short-term equity returns as earnings revisions turn upward across the index.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 5-day forward returns flips to negative over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal produces a Sharpe ratio below 0.1 in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, elevated borrowing costs suppress equity risk premiums and dampen leverage in corporate balance sheets, leading to negative forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive average returns in months where FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5%, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 4-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payroll growth coinciding with a stable unemployment rate confirms healthy labor demand, supporting consumer spending power and driving positive equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression over any 2-year window, or forward returns are significantly lower when both series improve simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery phases, real GDP crossing the 20 trillion dollar threshold signals an earnings inflection point, triggering rapid short-term equity gains as analyst revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: Equity returns decline when GDP crosses the 20 trillion threshold in the specified regime, or the signal fails to produce a positive Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.
